data.description:
## Why
Catch wiring bugs without a (near-undecidable, no-good-library) subsumption engine. Validated by
spike E2.

## What
- Extend `src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py` with a 3-state SHALLOW structural check: top-level
  required-field presence + primitive kind compatibility -> OK / UNKNOWN / ERROR; ABSTAIN (UNKNOWN)
  on `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf`/`not`/`$ref` (no subsumption).
- Add RUNTIME validation in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py`: a produced value is validated
  against the downstream CONSUMER's input contract via the registry validator (the real safety net).
- Editor: render an "unchecked" badge for UNKNOWN/opaque sources (`editor.py`).

## Scope
In: shallow static + runtime validation + badge. Out: MCP opaque handling (follow-on epic).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shallow check returns ERROR on missing-required / kind-mismatch, UNKNOWN on `oneOf`, OK on a matching shape (port spike E2).
- [ ] Runtime validation checks a produced value against the CONSUMER's input contract; a mismatch surfaces.
- [ ] The inspector shows an "unchecked" badge for UNKNOWN/opaque-typed sources.
data.title: Validation depth: 3-state shallow static + runtime-against-consumer
